articles.title: Chinese Consumers Tighten Purse Strings Despite Modest Income Gains
articles.summary:
- Chinese households are displaying their most cautious spending behavior since the peak of the pandemic, allocating the smallest share of their income to consumption
[para.
- This trend firmly underscores the immense challenge Beijing faces in its efforts to revive domestic demand against the backdrop of a protracted and severe property market slump
[para.
- The household propensity to consume, a critical indicator measuring the ratio of consumer spending to disposable income, fell to 64.6% in ...
